overall_reasoning: The patch precisely addresses the reported leak: antidote-script's git clone inherited stdout, so a noisy PATH wrapper injected text into generated plugin scripts. Redirecting clone stdout to stderr satisfies all three requirements (script commands stay on stdout, wrapper progress/diagnostics go to stderr, failures still propagate via the existing $? check). The regressions are focused, exercise the real boundary, and the trajectory shows the full 630-test suite passing with a clean, two-file commit. No blockers identified.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The fix is idiomatic zsh consistent with the surrounding code (stderr messaging already uses print -ru2 nearby), the intent is self-evident, and the test prose clearly describes each scenario.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Tests follow the repo's literate clitest markdown format, reuse mockgit, t_setup, t_reset, subenv, and $T_TEMPDIR conventions, and sit directly beside the existing kind:clone tests in test_cmd_script.md.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The change is exactly one redirection on the offending command plus two targeted test blocks; no unrelated refactoring, no broad suppression, and no speculative changes to other git call sites that don't feed generated scripts.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: No unsafe behavior: redirection cannot swallow errors, exit status is still checked, no eval or injection surface is introduced, and test shims only shadow git within the isolated test session.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Tests cover stdout purity, stderr capture of wrapper progress and diagnostics, and nonzero-failure propagation with an empty generated file; an initial clitest-incompatible multi-line function form was caught and corrected to a passing one-line form. Minor gap: no explicit assertion that git's own stderr diagnostics remain distinct from redirected stdout, but behavior is adequately covered.

strengths: Minimal one-line fix that redirects only the git clone invocation's stdout to stderr, exactly matching the task requirement
Exit status propagation preserved ($? checked immediately after the redirect; failure test asserts exit 1 and empty stdout file)
Regression tests exercise both a noisy-stdout wrapper and a failing wrapper at the actual generated-script boundary
Tests placed alongside existing antidote-script clone tests and use the repo's established mockgit/t_setup/clitest conventions
Full unit suite (630 tests) and focused suites pass; commit is clean and limited to the two intended files
summary: A git wrapper writing progress to stdout contaminated generated plugin scripts because `git clone` in antidote-script inherited stdout. The patch appends `>&2` to the clone command, keeping script commands on stdout and wrapper output on stderr while preserving failure propagation, and adds two focused clitest regressions verified against the full suite.
